fix: prevent test suite timeout caused by real network calls in force-overwrite test (#20428)

* Initial plan

* chore: initial plan for fixing test timeout in TestFetchDispatchWorkflows_ForceOverwritesConflict

Co-authored-by: pelikhan <4175913+pelikhan@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent TestFetchDispatchWorkflows_ForceOverwritesConflict from blocking on real network calls

Add variadic fileDownloadFn parameter to fetchAndSaveRemoteDispatchWorkflows for
testability, allowing tests to inject a fast-failing mock instead of making real
network calls that block for ~3 minutes in CI.

The test TestFetchDispatchWorkflows_ForceOverwritesConflict uses force=true, which
bypasses the conflict check and proceeds to DownloadFileFromGitHub. In CI this
blocks ~3 minutes (raw URL 30s timeout + git archive + git clone), causing the
entire test suite to time out at 3m0s.

test for this situation.

Switch the seasonal outlook flow to use ensemble-mean variables returned by the API instead of aggregating per-member values. Changes include:

Switch seasonal outlook from ensemble daily max/min to ensemble monthly mean values and propagate that change through parsing, store logic, UI and tests.

These changes align the app with the API's monthly-mean variables and consolidate theming/styling for Settings modal.

commit.message:
Fix GCC 13 Release build test failures (#7364) (#8176)

GCC 13 (e.g. 13.3.0 shipped with Ubuntu 24.04) has a known
miscompilation bug in -funswitch-loops
(https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=109934) that causes
widespread test failures when building DXC at -O3.

Disable -funswitch-loops for GCC 13, and update the CI pipeline from
Ubuntu-22.04 to Ubuntu-24.04 (which ships GCC 13 as default), removing
the workaround from #7365.

Fixes #7364

commit.message:
Merge pull request #129 from dchu3/fix/x402-v1-network-name

fix: use v1 legacy network names in x402 payment requirements

The server was sending x402Version: 1 with CAIP-2 network identifiers
(e.g. 'solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp'). The x402 client SDK's
v1 scheme registrations only match legacy names ('solana', 'solana-devnet'),
causing 'No supported payment requirements found' errors. The payment
failure leaked to Gemini, which then tried manual USDC transfers via
send_usdc instead of using the x402 protocol.

Now the payment requirements use v1 legacy names for the network field
while still using CAIP-2 for facilitator API calls (feePayer fetch).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use v1 legacy network names in x402 payment requirements

The server was sending x402Version: 1 with CAIP-2 network identifiers
(e.g. 'solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp'). The x402 client SDK's
v1 scheme registrations only match legacy names ('solana', 'solana-devnet'),
causing 'No supported payment requirements found' errors. The payment
failure leaked to Gemini, which then tried manual USDC transfers via
send_usdc instead of using the x402 protocol.

Now the payment requirements use v1 legacy names for the network field
while still using CAIP-2 for facilitator API calls (feePayer fetch).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.2.0

First public release

## Improved entity naming

Role entities now mirror the source entity's original name (e.g., "Summation Delivered" instead of "Sensor Energy"). Entity IDs follow HA's has_entity_name convention.

## Searchable device picker

The device selection dropdown is now a searchable combobox instead of a plain select. Supports filtering by entity domain.

## Options screen shows backing device

The configure dialog now displays which physical device backs the role, using the device's user-assigned name.

## Accumulator generalized to all cumulative sensors

The session accumulator now works with any total_increasing sensor — energy (kWh), water (m³), gas (ft³), and anything else that only goes up. Previously only energy sensors with kWh/Wh/MWh were supported. Values are stored in the source entity's native unit with no conversion.

## Bug fixes

- Accumulator routing fixed: sensors are now routed by state_class instead of device_class, preventing double-counting on cumulative energy sensors
- Display precision copied from source entity so values aren't rounded
- state_class copied from source instead of guessing from a hardcoded whitelist
- Unit mismatch on reassignment now rejected in the options flow instead of silently corrupting data
- Mid-session unit changes silently dropped instead of corrupting accumulator

## Breaking changes

- Existing role devices from v0.1.0 should be deleted and recreated. Entity class names and naming conventions changed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fixes #3985. Remove unix driver; augment ansi driver to support configurable size detection (#4821)

* Initial plan

* Fixes #3985. Remove unix driver; augment ansi driver to optionally support polling size detection

- Delete UnixComponentFactory, IUnixInput, UnixInput, UnixInputProcessor, UnixOutput
- Move shared Unix infrastructure (UnixIOHelper, UnixRawModeHelper, UnixTerminalHelper, SuspendHelper, UnixClipboard) to Drivers/ root
- Remove Names.UNIX and UNIX driver registration from DriverRegistry
- Update GetDefaultDriver() to return 'ansi' on Unix/macOS instead of 'unix'
- Add SizeDetectionMode enum (Polling/AnsiQuery)
- Add Driver.SizeDetection ConfigurationProperty defaulting to Polling
- Update AnsiComponentFactory.CreateSizeMonitor() to use config-based selection
- Register SizeDetectionMode in SourceGenerationContext for AOT
- Remove Unix driver tests for deleted classes
- Update all references to Names.UNIX and UnixComponentFactory

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix size detection: AnsiQuery default, SizeMonitorImpl init fix, Polling uses NativeSizeQuery, update docs and tests

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: use IOException in CreateNativeSizeQuery catch block with trace logging

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: ANSI driver now detects terminal resizes correctly

Previously, the ANSI driver was stuck at 80×25 and did not respond to terminal resize events due to using SizeMonitorImpl, which only saw a cached size. This commit ensures AnsiSizeMonitor is always used for AnsiOutput, regardless of SizeDetectionMode, enabling correct resize detection via CSI 18t queries. Added Trace.Lifecycle instrumentation for event/trace observability. Updated and added comprehensive tests for monitor behavior and trace output. Also restored and cleaned up clipboard, IO, and Windows VT helper files. Documentation details the bug, fix, and verification steps. The ANSI driver now reliably tracks terminal size changes on all supported platforms.

* Move SuspendHelper.cs to UnixHelpers directory

SuspendHelper contains only Unix libc P/Invoke (killpg, uname) for SIGTSTP.
It belongs in UnixHelpers alongside the other Unix-specific helpers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract flush P/Invoke from AnsiTerminalHelper to platform helpers

Move tcdrain/fsync to UnixIOHelper.FlushStdout() and
FlushFileBuffers to WindowsVTOutputHelper.FlushStdout().
Add isatty to UnixIOHelper for Phase 3.
AnsiTerminalHelper is now a pure dispatcher with zero DllImport.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract IsAttachedToTerminal P/Invoke to platform helpers

Create WindowsConsoleHelper with GetStdHandle/GetConsoleMode.
Driver.IsAttachedToTerminal dispatches to WindowsConsoleHelper
and UnixIOHelper.IsTerminal; contains zero DllImport itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move NetWinVTConsole to WindowsHelpers directory

NetWinVTConsole uses different console mode flags than
WindowsVTInputHelper (more conservative), so it cannot be
deleted as a straight replacement. Move it to WindowsHelpers
to consolidate all Win32 P/Invoke in the correct directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Code cleanup

Reformat and reorganize code for readability

Refactored several files to improve code readability and maintainability by reformatting constructor initializations, Bash command invocations, and P/Invoke declarations. No functional changes were made; all updates are organizational and stylistic.

* Improve suspend/resume: add cooked mode restore and diagnostics

Restore terminal to cooked mode (stty sane) before sending SIGTSTP so the
shell can function while the process is stopped. Add diagnostic logging to
the suspend/resume lifecycle and fix the killpg P/Invoke declaration
(SetLastError, parameter name).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix termios struct layout mismatch on macOS

The Termios P/Invoke struct used uint (4 bytes) for tcflag_t/speed_t and
NCCS=32, which matches Linux (56 bytes total) but is wrong for macOS where
tcflag_t/speed_t are unsigned long (8 bytes) and NCCS=20 (72 bytes total).
This caused a buffer overflow in tcgetattr and corrupted terminal state on
restore.

Replace the struct with a raw IntPtr buffer (128 bytes) since we only need
to save and restore the blob opaquely — no individual fields are read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

New commands:

  Installs all skills from pkg/skills/ and stores hooks from pkg/hooks/
  into env/.hooks/<package-name>/. Reports skill and hook counts.

  Unmerges tagged entries and removes symlinked scripts.

- skenv hooks list [--env <name>]
  Shows installed hook packages with their events and source paths.

Design: skills are user-scoped (managed globally by skenv), hooks are
project-scoped (applied explicitly per-project via hooks apply). This
avoids surprise writes to project configs while supporting multi-package
merge/unmerge with proper conflict handling.

commit.message:
fix(pi-gen): mask NetworkManager + userconfig.service to fix hotspot v0.4.3

Root cause found via Parallels VM image inspection of v0.4.1:
NetworkManager was installed and enabled by Raspberry Pi OS Bookworm by
default, silently taking over wlan0 in client/managed mode before hostapd
could start the AP. This was the primary reason the LogFalcon hotspot
never appeared after reboot.

Additionally userconfig.service (first-boot wizard) was being re-enabled
by pi-gen's export-image stage installing userconf-pi AFTER our custom
stage ran, overwriting our rm -f fix.

Fixes:
- Mask NetworkManager, NetworkManager-wait-online, ModemManager (all → /dev/null)
- Remove existing NM enable symlinks from wants directories
- Add /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/logfalcon-unmanaged.conf marking wlan0/usb0
  as unmanaged (belt-and-suspenders for any future NM un-masking)
- Mask userconfig.service with /dev/null symlink (survives export-image reinstall)
- logfalcon-wifi-init.service now runs Before=systemd-networkd.service too

Bump to v0.4.3; update CHANGELOG, README, docs.

commit.message:
fix: throttle popover scroll handlers and scope ChatPopup resize listeners (#3177)

* Initial plan

* fix: throttle popover scroll handlers with RAF and scope ChatPopup resize listeners

Root cause: Layout thrashing from unthrottled getBoundingClientRect() + setState()
calls in scroll event handlers caused the main thread to stall during rapid scroll,
producing intermittent scroll freezing.

- Wrap scroll/resize position recalculations in requestAnimationFrame across 5
  components (NotificationBell, AddAgentPopover, ChoreCard, ModelSelector, StageCard)
  to coalesce layout reads to once per frame
- Move ChatPopup's window-level mousemove/mouseup listeners from always-on to
  only active during resize operations, eliminating unnecessary event dispatching

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add tests for scroll handler throttling and resize listener scoping

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge judge changes into copilot/fix-scroll-freezing-issue

* Initial plan

* review: adopt justified PR feedback

- Wrap flushRAF() callback execution in act() to suppress React state-update warnings and prevent flaky assertions

Evaluated against PR review recommendations.
